free_text: Small-angle proton elastic scattering from the
neutron-rich isotopes 6-He and 8-He and from 4-He at
p + 4He scattering at the proton energy Ep = 695 MeV
The detector IKAR consists of six in-line axial
ionization chambers and has a 2-pi acceptance in
azimuthal angle for the recoil proton registration.
The main constituent is the ionization chamber IKAR,
described in detail in REL-REF, filled with hydrogen
gas at a pressure of 10 bar. Beam particles interacted
with the protons inside IKAR, which thus served
simultaneously as a target and a recoil proton
detector. The recoil protons registered in IKAR were
measured in coincidence with the scattered
He particles. For the purpose of determining the
scattering angle of the He particles, a system of
tracking detectors was applied. It consisted of two
The helium ions produced by fragmentation of
18-O entered FRS and were separated according to their
magnetic rigidity. The beam intensities were 1000
1/sec, with the duty factor between 25% and 50%.
By authors. The indicated energies correspond to the
equivalent proton energies (in the laboratory frame)
for the case of direct kinematics.
